index.title: Ripple — Performance Physical Therapy in Boston
index.description: Performance Physical Therapy for active lifestyles in Boston. Keep doing what you love, no matter how hard you push it.

blocks.eyebrow: Performance Physical Therapy for Active Lifestyles in Boston
blocks.headline: Keep doing what you love, no matter how hard you push it
blocks.subhead: We fix the root cause of your injury so you can get back to the active lifestyle that makes you you.

blocks.headline: Injuries strip you of your identity
blocks.body: When pain keeps you from the things you love — running, lifting, climbing, playing with your kids — it takes more than your mobility. It takes a piece of who you are. Generic stretches and band-aid fixes won't get it back.

blocks.headline: We care about your injuries as much as you do
blocks.tagline: We push it too, and we know how hard it is when you can't.

steps.title: Book a FREE phone consultation
steps.body: Hop on a quick 15-minute call so we can understand your goals, your injury, and whether we're the right fit.

steps.title: Determine who your guide is
steps.body: We match you with the therapist who specializes in exactly what you need and build a plan around your life.

steps.title: Get back & better to your active lifestyle
steps.body: Execute the plan with a coach in your corner, and return stronger than before — without fear of re-injury.

blocks.headline: You lose a piece of yourself when you give up your active lifestyle
blocks.body: Performance Physical Therapy doesn't just dull the pain — it eliminates it at the source, so you can keep pushing without holding back.

items.title: Performance Physical Therapy
items.body: We don't just put a band-aid on your injury — we fix the root cause. 60-minute one-on-one sessions combining manual therapy, dry needling, and strength work.

items.title: Latest Technology
items.body: Force dynamometry, run analysis, blood flow restriction, and golf swing assessments — data-driven care that finds what's really going on.

items.title: Ripple Virtual
items.body: Personalized Zoom sessions, online coaching, and a mobile app — expert care wherever you are, on your schedule.

blocks.headline: We've helped hundreds of Boston residents get back to what they love
blocks.body: It starts with a free consultation and a customized plan. From there, you execute with us in your corner — follow-up emails after every session, weekly exercises delivered through our app, and 24/7 access to your clinician.
items: Perform better than before your injury
Never fear re-injury holding you back

blocks.headline: See what we're all about
blocks.body: A space built for performance and a team that treats your goals like their own. Let's find out if we're the right fit.

blocks.headline: Got tight hips?
blocks.body: Most advice tells you to stretch more. We'll show you the customized approach that actually loosens tight hips for good — free guide, no strings.

physical-therapy.title: Physical Therapy — Ripple
physical-therapy.description: Performance Physical Therapy in Boston: 60-minute one-on-one sessions that fix the root cause of your pain.

blocks.headline: We fix the root cause — not just the symptom
blocks.subhead: Every session is 60 minutes, one-on-one with your therapist. No double-booking, no passing you to an aide.

blocks.headline: What makes Performance PT different
body: Most clinics treat the spot that hurts and send you on your way. We look at how your whole body moves, find why the pain started, and build the strength and mechanics that keep it from coming back.
That means more time with you, better assessments, and a plan built around the life you actually want to live.

items.title: Hands-on manual therapy
items.body: Targeted soft-tissue and joint work to restore mobility and calm down painful tissue fast.

items.title: Dry needling
items.body: Precise relief for stubborn muscular pain and trigger points that won't let go.

items.title: Strength & performance
items.body: Real gym-based work that rebuilds capacity so your fix holds under load.

items.title: Force dynamometry
items.body: Objective strength testing so we measure progress instead of guessing at it.

items.title: Running & gait analysis
items.body: Slow-motion video and metrics to find the mechanics behind your injury.

items.title: Blood flow restriction
items.body: Build strength with lighter loads when you're early in recovery or rehabbing.

steps.title: Assess
steps.body: A full-body evaluation to find the true source of your pain — not just where you feel it.

steps.title: Treat
steps.body: Hands-on care plus a personalized plan you can actually fit into your week.

steps.title: Build
steps.body: Progressive strength work so you come back more durable than you were before.

blocks.headline: Conditions we treat every day
blocks.body: If pain is keeping you from the activities you love, there's a good chance we can help. A few of the most common reasons people come to Ripple:
items: Running injuries (runner's knee, IT band, shin splints)

blocks.headline: Not sure if we're the right fit?
blocks.body: That's exactly what the free 15-minute call is for. No pressure — just answers.

about.title: About Us — Ripple
about.description: Meet the Ripple team — performance physical therapists who push it too.

blocks.headline: We push it too
blocks.subhead: We're athletes, lifters, runners, and climbers first — and we know exactly how it feels when an injury takes that away.

blocks.headline: Our story
body: Ripple started with a simple frustration: traditional physical therapy wasn't built for people who push their bodies. Fifteen-minute visits, hot packs, and a sheet of generic exercises don't cut it when your goal is a marathon, a max lift, or a hard climb.
So we built something different — a clinic where every session is an hour, one-on-one, focused on getting you back to performing, not just pain-free on paper.

items.title: Root cause, always
items.body: We never settle for masking symptoms. We find why it started and fix it for good.

items.title: Performance, not just recovery
items.body: Our goal isn't to get you to zero pain — it's to get you better than before.

items.title: You're an athlete
items.body: However you move, we treat you like the athlete you are and train you like one.

items.title: Casey
items.body: Founder & Performance PT. Leads every new client's first consultation.

items.title: Jules
items.body: Specializes in low back and persistent pain with a personalized approach.

items.title: Chris
items.body: Adapts care across changing diagnoses to keep you moving forward.

items.title: Dan
items.body: Known for fast, measurable progress where others have stalled.

blocks.headline: Come see what we're all about
blocks.body: Let's find out if we're the right fit — starting with a free 15-minute call.

blog.title: Blog — Ripple
blog.description: Tips, guides, and stories from the Ripple Physical Therapy team.

blocks.headline: Move better, live better
blocks.subhead: Practical guidance from our team on training, recovery, and staying in the game.

posts.title: Why stretching isn't fixing your tight hips
posts.excerpt: If you've been stretching for months with no change, the problem probably isn't flexibility. Here's what actually works.
body: If your hips feel tight no matter how much you stretch, you're not alone — and you're probably chasing the wrong fix.

Tightness is often your body's way of protecting a joint that doesn't feel stable or strong through its full range. Stretch all you want; if the underlying strength and control aren't there, the tightness comes right back.

The real fix is usually a mix of mobility work in the positions you actually use, plus strength built through that new range so your body trusts it. That's exactly the kind of plan we build in a Performance PT session.

posts.title: Coming back from runner's knee without losing your season
posts.excerpt: Runner's knee doesn't have to mean months off. Here's how we get runners back on the road — smartly.
body: Runner's knee is one of the most common reasons runners come to see us — and one of the most fixable.
The key is to address the mechanics driving the pain rather than just resting until it quiets down. Rest alone tends to set you up for the same problem the moment mileage climbs again.
With the right load management, hip and quad strength work, and small gait tweaks, most runners are back to training in weeks, not months.
